org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge

Class PolylineEdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.AbstractPropertyTooler
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.view.AbstractViewComponent
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.AbstractFigure
          • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.AbstractModelObjectFigure<E>
            • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.EdgeFigure<E>
              • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.edge.PolylineEdgeFigure<E>
  • Type Parameters:
    E - is the type of the model edge supported by this figure.
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<ViewComponent>, Selectable, PropertyOwner, Figure, ModelObjectFigure<E>, LinearFeature, ModelObjectView<E>, ViewComponent, ViewComponentConstants, PropertyNames, View
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    FSMTransitionFigure

    public class PolylineEdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
extends EdgeFigure<E>
    Edge that is drawn with a polyline.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:01
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-fig

      Change the size of this figure to fit its content as best as possible.

      Try to remove the control point at the specified index if its removal does produce a large change in the polyline.

    • Constructor Detail

      • PolylineEdgeFigure

        public PolylineEdgeFigure(UUID viewUUID,
                  float xfrom,
                  float yfrom,
                  float xto,
                  float yto)
        Contruct a new SegmentEdgeFigure.
        Parameters:
        viewUUID - is the UUID of the view that is enclosing this figure.
        xfrom - horizontal position of the begin point for this FigEdge.
        yfrom - vertical position of the begin point for this FigEdge.
        xto - horizontal position of the end point for this FigEdge.
        yto - vertical position of the end point for this FigEdge.

      • PolylineEdgeFigure

        public PolylineEdgeFigure(UUID viewUUID)
        Contruct a new SegmentEdgeFigure.
        Parameters:
        viewUUID - is the UUID of the view that is enclosing this figure.

    • Method Detail

      • fitToContent

        public void fitToContent()
        Description copied from interface: Figure
        Change the size of this figure to fit its content as best as possible.

      • getMaxAllowedCtrlPoints

        public int getMaxAllowedCtrlPoints()
        Return the max number of control point for the edge. Usually two for a linear edge, and Interger.MAX_VALUE for a poly edge.
        Specified by:
        getMaxAllowedCtrlPoints in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        an integer that is the max count of control points.

      • computePath

        protected EdgeFigure.PathDetails computePath(List<? extends Point2D> controlPoints)
        Create and replies the general path that is representing this edge figure. It is recommended to invoke EdgeFigure.getPath() instead of this method.
        Specified by:
        computePath in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        controlPoints - are the control points that are composing the path.
        Returns:
        the general path.

      • setDrawingMethod

        public void setDrawingMethod(DrawingMethod method)
        Set the method how the polyline edge is drawn.
        Parameters:
        method - is the type of the drawning method.

      • getDrawingMethod

        public DrawingMethod getDrawingMethod()
        Replies the method how the polyline edge is drawn.
        Returns:
        the method used to draw this edge.

        public Map<String,Object> getProperties()
        Specified by:
        getProperties in interface PropertyOwner
        Overrides:
        getProperties in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>

        public Map<String,Class<?>> getUIEditableProperties()
        Replies the definitions of the properties that may be edited through an UI.

        getUIEditableProperties in interface Figure
        Overrides:

        Returns:

      • setProperties

        public void setProperties(Map<String,Object> properties)
        Specified by:
        setProperties in interface PropertyOwner
        Overrides:
        setProperties in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>

      • computeSegmentLengths

        protected void computeSegmentLengths(float[] lengths)
        Compute the lengths of all the segments and put these lengths in the specified array. The size of the specified array must be the same as the number of control points
        Overrides:
        computeSegmentLengths in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        lengths - are the computed lengths.

      • hitSegment

        public int hitSegment(float x,
             float y,
             float epsilon)
        Replies the index of the nearest segment to the specified point.
        Specified by:
        hitSegment in interface LinearFeature
        Overrides:
        hitSegment in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        epsilon - is the maximal distance from the point to the segment.
        Returns:
        the index of the nearest segment on the edge. It is always in [-1,LinearFeature.getCtrlPointCount()-2], where the value -1 is when there is no segment in the edge.
        See Also:
        LinearFeature.hitCtrlPoint(float, float, float)

      • getNearestSegmentTo

        public int getNearestSegmentTo(float x,
                      float y)
        Replies the index of the nearest segment to the specified point.
        Specified by:
        getNearestSegmentTo in interface LinearFeature
        Overrides:
        getNearestSegmentTo in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        the index of the nearest segment on the edge. It is always in [-1,LinearFeature.getCtrlPointCount()-2], where the value -1 is when there is no segment in the edge.
        See Also:
        LinearFeature.getNearestCtrlPointTo(float, float)

      • flatteningAt

        public boolean flatteningAt(int index)
        Try to remove the control point at the specified index if its removal does produce a large change in the polyline. This function is useful to rmeove the control points that do not contribute enough to the shape of the edge.
        Specified by:
        flatteningAt in interface LinearFeature
        Specified by:
        flatteningAt in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Returns:
        true if the control point was removed; false if it was not removed.

      • isClosedToSegment

        protected boolean isClosedToSegment(float x1,
                        float y1,
                        float x2,
                        float y2,
                        float x,
                        float y,
                        float hitDistance)
        Replies if a point is closed to a segment. By default this function calls MathUtil.isPointClosedToSegment(float, float, float, float, float, float, float). This function may be overridden by the subclasses to influence the EdgeFigure.hitSegment(float, float, float)'s behavior.
        Specified by:
        isClosedToSegment in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        x1 - horizontal location of the first-segment begining.
        y1 - vertical location of the first-segment ending.
        x2 - horizontal location of the second-segment begining.
        y2 - vertical location of the second-segment ending.
        x - horizontal location of the point.
        y - vertical location of the point.
        hitDistance - is the maximal hitting distance.
        Returns:
        true if the point and the line have closed locations.

      • distanceToSegment

        protected float distanceToSegment(float x1,
                      float y1,
                      float x2,
                      float y2,
                      float x,
                      float y,
                      Point2D pts)
        Replies if a point is closed to a segment. By default this function calls MathUtil.distancePointToSegment(float, float, float, float, float, float). This function may be overridden by the subclasses to influence the EdgeFigure.getNearestSegmentTo(float, float)'s behavior.
        Specified by:
        distanceToSegment in class EdgeFigure<E extends Edge<?,?,?,?>>
        Parameters:
        x1 - horizontal location of the first-segment begining.
        y1 - vertical location of the first-segment ending.
        x2 - horizontal location of the second-segment begining.
        y2 - vertical location of the second-segment ending.
        x - horizontal location of the point.
        y - vertical location of the point.
        pts - is set with the coordinates of the nearest point, if not null.
        Returns:
        true if the point and the line have closed locations.
